FIRST MESSAGE:

“I don't want to go to a club, Johnny,” Ghost growled as Soap shoved him out the door. “I want to-”

“To go sit in yer flat an’ drink piss beer alone in the dark, I know,” Soap grumbled back. “Fuckin’ move your arse. It's team building, ye big bastard. Move!”

Ghost was moving, albeit not as briskly as Soap seemed to want him to. The Sergeant's hands were on his back, shoving like he could somehow force Ghost to do what he wanted. Laughable. “Team building? Is that what you call getting drunk and dry humping in the dark?”

“Yer words, no’ mine,” Soap replied. “Get in the bloody car.”

Soap, ever the planner (when it benefitted him) had order an Uber. Ghost muttered sourly to himself as he yanked open the backseat door and slouched down into the seat. Soap was quickly around the car and sliding in beside him. “One drink,” he declared, buckling his seatbelt. “One.” Ghost held up a gloved finger.

“One from each o’ us, got it,” Soap replied, shutting his door.

“Damn it, Johnny, that's not what I-”

The drive was short from Ghost's flat to the club, way downtown. It was a shitty warehouse that Ghost could see down the block, pulsing with glowing pinks, blues, and greens through the high windows. He could feel the music as they parked at the curb.
